subject: [PATCH 5/8] nfsd: widen nfsd_genl_rqstp address fields to sockaddr_storage

2026-06-01.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the nfsd kernel module where IPv6 addresses were being truncated when sent to userspace via netlink attributes. The issue was caused by casting a 16-byte sockaddr field to a 28-byte sockaddr_in6 pointer, which extended beyond the end of the field into an adjacent member. To fix this, the patch widens the address fields in the nfsd_genl_rqstp struct from sockaddr to sockaddr_storage, ensuring that IPv6 addresses are captured fully and preventing uninitialized bytes from being leaked through netlink.
